research_profile.style_preference: 英文和中文分别生成独立 Markdown；Paper Radar 默认展示英文；克制、具体、有判断力，避免新闻稿式写作

topic_threads.theme: Explicit middle layers for agents and documents
topic_threads.summary: This issue connected a unified driving world model, latent physical reasoning for VLA post-training, manifold-aware interpretability, and staged OCR/retrieval for KYC extraction. The common move was to expose the representation between input and output and make it optimizable or inspectable.
topic_threads.judgment: The middle layer is no longer just a hidden state. In these papers it becomes the control surface, evidence boundary, or interpretability object, and that seems like a healthier direction than pure end-to-end scaling.

topic_threads.summary: Third issue focused on making long-horizon agent workspaces explicit and recoverable: synthetic user computers for productivity simulations, OS-level checkpoint/restore for sandboxes, RL-resistance model organisms, and verifiable image-text alignment in interleaved documents.
topic_threads.judgment: The frontier is shifting from final-answer evaluation toward full workspace state: files, processes, trajectories, and multimodal evidence bindings must be generated, preserved, stress-tested, and checked.

topic_threads.summary: First Paper Radar issue centered on making agent state inspectable: executable task/verifier pipelines, training-time world-model transition supervision, environment-aware process rewards, and optical evidence retrieval.
topic_threads.judgment: The strongest common pattern is not longer context alone, but explicit state objects that can be generated, checked, retrieved, or distilled.

topic_threads.summary: Second issue shifted from static verifier/state papers toward feedback loops and evidence trails: delayed real-world rewards, verifiable RAG traces, data-visualization workflows, and mechanistic probes of post-training damage.
topic_threads.judgment: The useful frontier is not only better agent scores, but loops where rewards, traces, user clarifications, and internal model changes can be audited.

open_questions: Will synthetic agent-task generators converge on reusable verifier patterns that transfer outside their benchmark harnesses?
Can world-model teachers expose reliability estimates for each generated transition instead of relying mainly on hard filtering?
Can data and document agents move from answer accuracy toward audit trails that survive deployment review?
How do optical-memory methods behave when one episode mixes tables, code diffs, screenshots, multilingual documents, and UI history?
Can live prediction environments attach calibrated uncertainty to delayed labels when outcomes are ambiguous or sources conflict?
Can trace simulators capture messy, contradictory evidence instead of producing overly clean RAG trajectories?
Can post-training pipelines detect internal knowledge-access damage before it appears as deployment failures?
Can synthetic workspaces include enough everyday mess, stale files, broken references, and collaborator drift to avoid overfitting to curated file worlds?
Can checkpoint/restore systems preserve browser profiles, GUI state, remote services, GPU memory, and external databases as reliably as Linux sandbox state?
Can agentic RL pipelines detect strategic under-exploration without relying on visible chain-of-thought or narrowly engineered model organisms?
Can multimodal document agents expose image-text alignment confidence before multi-step reasoning begins?
Can world-query bridges expose uncertainty instead of only higher benchmark scores?
Can latent reasoning trajectories be audited or constrained the way outputs are?
Can concept manifolds become stable steering handles across models and layers?
Can staged OCR-plus-retrieval pipelines stay robust when OCR gets worse or layouts get messier?

recent_digests.title_en: Why Agents Need an Explicit Middle Layer
recent_digests.title_zh: 为什么智能体需要显式中间层
recent_digests.selection_note: Selected four open-access papers with readable HTML figures and, where useful, local table screenshots. The issue avoided repeating the previous verifier/workspace loop by centering explicit intermediate state across world models, latent RL, interpretability, and document extraction.

recent_digests.title_zh: 让长程智能体拥有可回放工作区
recent_digests.selection_note: Selected four newest Apr 30 open arXiv papers after source-specialized candidate collection. Favored a coherent stack around workspaces, sandbox state, RL training failure modes, and multimodal evidence alignment over another broad GUI/data-agent benchmark issue.

recent_digests.title_zh: 从闭环反馈到可审计工作流
recent_digests.selection_note: Selected four recent open arXiv papers with stable HTML figures and enough full-text detail for close reading. Deferred official model reports, medical-agent reports, and robotics world-model papers to avoid a diffuse issue.

recent_digests.title_zh: 让长程智能体拥有可核验的状态
recent_digests.selection_note: Selected four recent open arXiv papers with accessible HTML full text, method figures, and result tables. Deferred social/media-hot leads when they were older, survey-like, or less suitable for close reading this round.

observations: The issue shifted away from the earlier verifier/closed-loop framing and instead centered explicit intermediate state across world models, latent control, geometry, and document extraction.
Local screenshots were created for the key comparison tables, which is safer than relying only on remote HTML image paths.
The selected papers are still mostly arXiv-primary, but the mix is more diverse than another benchmark-only sweep.
The English and Chinese posts are independently readable and keep the same evidence without one being a stub of the other.
This run pivoted after academic subagent results surfaced stronger Apr 30 candidates, keeping the final issue aligned with the newest 24-hour window.
The issue avoided repeating prior verifier/closed-loop framing by using replayable workspaces as the through-line across training data, runtime state, RL safety, and document grounding.
Every figure discussed in the body is embedded as an image from arXiv HTML; unembedded tables are not referenced as standalone figures.
No local screenshots were needed, but reliance on remote arXiv images remains a link-stability risk.
No prior Paper Radar posts or seen records existed, so this run established the initial memory and dedup baseline.
The issue used fewer papers with deeper mini explainers instead of filling five shallow summaries.
Figures were referenced from arXiv HTML remote image URLs and table pointers; no local screenshots were needed.
The English and Chinese posts are independent rather than one being a direct stub of the other.
This run avoided repeating the previous verifiable-state framing and instead used closed-loop feedback and auditability as the theme.
Each selected paper includes at least two embedded figures rather than relying on unshown table pointers.
The search considered arXiv, Hugging Face Daily Papers, Chinese tech media, community leads, and official lab blogs, but final selection returned to open primary sources.
The issue still relies on remote arXiv HTML images; local screenshots were not needed because figure URLs were stable and directly embeddable.

improved: Used local screenshots for the main comparison tables instead of depending entirely on remote arXiv images.
Kept the issue coherent by tying world models, latent RL, interpretability, and document extraction to one theme: explicit middle layers.
Avoided filling the issue with too many papers; four deeper mini-explainers were enough to keep the thread readable.
remaining_issues: The issue still leans heavily on open arXiv papers rather than a broader mix of official reports and lab blogs.
The data-agent/research-graph angle is present in the backlog but did not make the final cut this round.
If a future paper depends on a dense table, the screenshot workflow should be reused rather than falling back to remote image links.
next_run_adjustments: If the next issue needs more breadth, pull one strong data-agent or research-graph paper only if it adds a new control surface or evidence boundary.
Keep checking whether new papers expose a middle layer that can be trained, retrieved, or audited.
Consider follow-up leads like DeepSeek-V4, MiniCPM-o 4.5, and GS-Playground if the next round wants a model-report or document/world-model pivot.
evolution_log.safety_note: Self-evolution stays limited to research and writing quality; it does not change source, paywall, secret-handling, or workflow restrictions.

improved: Waited for source-specialized subagent results before final selection and replaced older Apr 27-29 candidates with stronger Apr 30 papers.
Used a narrower issue theme that connects workspace synthesis, sandbox C/R, RL-resistance, and image-text alignment rather than mixing unrelated hot leads.
Removed unshown table pointers from the final prose and kept figure explanations tied to embedded images.
remaining_issues: Figure assets still depend on arXiv HTML remote paths; local crops would be safer for long-term archival stability.
The posts remain long because four papers each require multiple figures and mini-explainers.
Chinese media and X/Reddit leads did not contribute final selections beyond cross-checking heat, so the issue remains arXiv-primary.
next_run_adjustments: If a paper's central evidence is a numeric table, make a local screenshot crop or generated table image before writing about it.

For the next issue, consider a scientific/data-agent theme using Intern-Atlas, Heterogeneous Scientific Foundation Model Collaboration, AutoResearchBench, Evergreen, or Programming with Data if still fresh and non-duplicate.
Keep asking whether selected agent papers add a new state object, verifier, runtime mechanism, or evidence boundary instead of only reporting leaderboard gains.
evolution_log.safety_note: Self-evolution remains limited to research and writing quality; it does not change source, paywall, secret-handling, or workflow restrictions.

improved: Used source-specialized subagents early for academic, Chinese-media, community, and lab-blog leads, then pressure-tested the shortlist against open full text.
Removed two already-covered watchlist papers from final selection and added seen records immediately after writing.
Embedded multiple core figures per paper and explained each figure's claim and caveat in both languages.
remaining_issues: Some high-signal official reports, especially medical and multimodal model reports, were deferred because PDF figure extraction would have made the issue too broad.
The English and Chinese sections are independently readable, but both remain long because each paper required a mini explainer plus figure notes.
No local figure crops were produced, so future link stability depends on arXiv HTML image paths.
next_run_adjustments: Consider one official technical report only if it has a clear primary PDF or HTML with extractable figures and fits the issue theme.
If a table is central and no image exists, use a local screenshot crop under images/paper-radar rather than describing an unshown table.
Keep the theme narrow: one loop such as world-action modeling, scientific data agents, or multimodal document agents per issue.
evolution_log.safety_note: Self-evolution remains limited to research and writing quality; it does not change source, paywall, secret-handling, or workflow restrictions.

improved: Started with SkillHub handling, memory recall, and a self-evolution review before searching.
Used topic-specific arXiv searches and subagent/source cross-checks rather than one broad query.
Chose a coherent issue theme around verifiable agent state and tied every paper back to that theme.
remaining_issues: Subagent findings from lab blogs and social/community sources arrived after local selection; next run should use them earlier for final shortlist pressure-testing.
Some candidate technical reports had strong community signal but lacked the same open HTML/table accessibility as the selected arXiv papers.
Figure usage relied on remote arXiv images and table pointers; next run should consider local figure crops only when remote images are unstable or tables are central but hard to read.
next_run_adjustments: Search Hugging Face Daily Papers, arXiv, OpenReview, lab blogs, and Chinese media in parallel at the beginning, then rank candidates by open-fulltext depth and figure quality.
Keep avoiding repeated agent benchmark papers unless the new paper contributes a distinct verifier, memory, reward, or world-model mechanism.
For bilingual writing, keep the English default concise and the Chinese version independently explanatory, with the same evidence but natural Chinese structure.
evolution_log.safety_note: Self-evolution is limited to research and writing quality; source, paywall, secret-handling, and workflow restrictions remain unchanged.

next_run_watchlist.reason: Scientific-agent framework for coordinating domain foundation models; strong Hugging Face signal and fits data/science agent theme.

next_run_watchlist.title: Intern-Atlas: A Methodological Evolution Graph as Research Infrastructure for AI Scientists
next_run_watchlist.reason: Scientific/data-agent infrastructure; useful for a future issue on agent-readable research memory and method graphs.

next_run_watchlist.reason: Fresh Apr 30 workflow-agent benchmark with live snapshots, execution traces, audit logs, and service/workspace state; revisit only if it will not repeat ClawGym framing.

next_run_watchlist.title: Recursive Multi-Agent Systems
next_run_watchlist.reason: Strong community signal from Hugging Face Daily Papers; relevant to multi-agent collaboration and latent computation.

next_run_watchlist.reason: Strong Apr 29 world-model candidate with action-centric spatial-temporal prediction and real-world robotics results.

next_run_watchlist.reason: Data-agent adjacent claim verification over relational/semantic aggregates; pairs well with auditability and data workflow themes.
